title: Resolve merge conflicts for Exclude investigator beads from corrective bead creation
description:
Merge conflict detected during preflight merge of main into feature/b-e622b3eb.
Conflicted files: .takt/beads/B-e622b3eb.json

Git error: Auto-merging .takt/beads/B-e622b3eb.json
CONFLICT (add/add): Merge conflict in .takt/beads/B-e622b3eb.json
Automatic merge failed; fix conflicts and then commit the result.

Diff context:
diff --cc .takt/beads/B-e622b3eb.json
index 0da6b1d,f951e67..0000000
--- a/.takt/beads/B-e622b3eb.json
+++ b/.takt/beads/B-e622b3eb.json
@@@ -3,7 -3,7 +3,11 @@@
    "title": "Exclude investigator beads from corrective bead creation",
    "agent_type": "developer",
    "description": "When an investigator bead is blocked, the scheduler incorrectly creates a corrective developer bead to fix it. Corrective beads make no sense for investigator beads \u2014 they are read-only analysis tasks, not implementation work, and a developer corrective cannot meaningfully unblock a failed investigation (which should simply be retried or requeued).\n\nFix `_can_plan_corrective` in `src/agent_takt/scheduler/followups.py` to return `False` when the bead's `agent_type` is `\"investigator\"`. Add the check at the top of the method alongside the existing corrective/recovery exclusions:\n\n```python\ndef _can_plan_corrective(self, bead: Bead) -> bool:\n    if self._is_corrective_bead(bead):\n        return False\n    if bead.agent_type == \"investigator\":\n        return False\n    ...\n```\n\nAlso add a test in the scheduler followup tests verifying that a blocked investigator bead does not trigger corrective bead creation.",
++<<<<<<< HEAD
 +  "status": "ready",
++=======
+   "status": "done",
++>>>>>>> main
    "bead_type": "task",
    "parent_id": null,
    "dependencies": [],
@@@ -18,36 -18,52 +22,85 @@@
    "execution_branch_name": "feature/b-e622b3eb",
    "execution_worktree_path": "/Users/oscar.renalias/Projects/codex-agent-orchestration/.takt/worktrees/B-e622b3eb",
    "expected_files": [

 +    "src/agent_takt/scheduler/followups.py"
 +  ],
 +  "expected_globs": [],
 +  "touched_files": [],
 +  "changed_files": [],
 +  "updated_docs": [],

++=======

+     "tests/test_scheduler_followups.py"

+   "updated_docs": [],
+   "handoff_summary": {
+     "completed": "Updated `_can_plan_corrective` to return `False` for `investigator` beads before recovery/corrective planning continues. Added a scheduler followups test asserting a blocked investigator bead does not get an auto corrective child and leaves no `auto_corrective_bead_id` metadata. Verified both changed Python files pass `uv run python -m py_compile`.",
+     "remaining": "Tester should run the scheduler followup test suite and confirm existing corrective behavior for non-investigator agent types remains unchanged.",
+     "risks": "Low risk. The change narrows corrective eligibility for one agent type only; the main downstream check is confirming no existing scheduler flows relied on investigator corrective planning.",
+     "verdict": "approved",
+     "findings_count": 0,

+       "src/agent_takt/scheduler/followups.py",

handoff_summary.completed: Updated `.takt/beads/B-e622b3eb.json` from the stale `ready` state to the merged `done` state, including the completed handoff summary, touched/changed file scope, branch/worktree metadata, execution history, and metadata payload from the original feature completion.
handoff_summary.remaining: No code changes remain in this bead's scope. The only unavailable check here was `uv run`-based JSON validation because the sandbox denied access to `~/.cache/uv`.
handoff_summary.risks: Low risk. The change is confined to bead metadata, but downstream tooling still depends on the JSON remaining well-formed. Validation via `uv run` could not be completed in this sandbox.
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: This bead updates scheduler state metadata only. Any downstream agent reading feature-root status should now see `B-e622b3eb` as completed instead of `ready`.
handoff_summary.design_decisions: Chose to reconcile the file to the already-completed `main` version rather than preserve the local stale `ready` state, because the conflict description and branch history show the feature implementation had already finished and the merge issue was metadata divergence only.
handoff_summary.test_coverage_notes: N/A. No executable source changed in this bead. A non-test validation using `uv run` was attempted but blocked by sandbox access to the UV cache.
handoff_summary.known_limitations: `uv run` validation could not be executed in this environment due sandbox restrictions on `~/.cache/uv`, so correctness here is based on the reconciled diff and preserved JSON structure.

conflict_risks: This bead updates scheduler state metadata only. Any downstream agent reading feature-root status should now see `B-e622b3eb` as completed instead of `ready`.

execution_history.summary: Resolved the merge-conflict bead by reconciling `.takt/beads/B-e622b3eb.json` to the completed state already recorded on `main`, preserving the prior implementation handoff metadata and completed execution history.

guardrails.template_text:
# Developer Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Implement only the assigned bead inside the existing architecture and file scope.

Allowed actions:
- Modify code, configuration, or local docs required to complete the assigned bead.
- Create sub-beads for discovered follow-up work that should be handled separately.
- Verify your `{{LANGUAGE}}` changes compile and import correctly with a quick syntax check: `{{BUILD_CHECK_COMMAND}}`.

Disallowed actions:
- Run any test suite — not even a targeted `{{TEST_COMMAND}}` run or any subset. Test execution is exclusively the tester agent's responsibility. Running tests wastes the agent budget and delays the pipeline.
- Redesign unrelated architecture or broaden scope beyond the assigned bead.
- Perform final review signoff in place of a review agent.
- Silently absorb unrelated test, documentation, or planning work that should be handed off.

Required Handoff Fields:

Every developer bead **must** populate the following three fields in its output JSON. Reviewers and tester agents rely on these fields to scope their work without additional back-and-forth turns. Use `"N/A"` only when a field is genuinely inapplicable (e.g., a trivial rename with no design trade-offs); do not leave fields blank.

- **`design_decisions`** — Document non-obvious architectural or implementation choices made during this bead. Include alternatives considered and why the chosen approach was preferred. Reviewers use this to evaluate correctness without re-deriving intent. Set to `"N/A"` only if the change was purely mechanical with no meaningful trade-offs.

- **`test_coverage_notes`** — Describe what the tester agent should verify: new code paths, edge cases introduced, regression risks, and any known untested scenarios left to the tester. Set to `"N/A"` only if the bead touched no executable code paths (e.g., documentation-only changes).

- **`known_limitations`** — Call out constraints, deferred work, or known gaps in the implementation. This includes out-of-scope items deliberately left for follow-up beads and any areas where the implementation is intentionally incomplete. Set to `"N/A"` if the implementation is complete as specified with no known gaps.

Expected outputs:
- Completed or blocked JSON with concise implementation summary.
- Accurate touched files, changed files, risks, and follow-up handoff fields.
- Populated `design_decisions`, `test_coverage_notes`, and `known_limitations` fields in every response.
